metadata.decision_rule: at K=10, evidence-weighted: ALL true alternatives recovered in dedup top-K AND ranked strictly above every decoy candidate AND outrank the random-walk baseline's best recovered score for a true alternative (trivially satisfied if baseline never recovers one)
metadata.dedup_simplification: exact joined-vertex-label string match only (no SHA-256 / near-duplicate clustering); valid here because true/decoy arms are vertex-disjoint by construction
metadata.peak_rss_mib_semantics: peak_rss_mib records process-level Sys.maxrss() sampled once per (graph_id, weighting, K) k_shortest_paths call; it is process-wide provenance, not per-algorithm incremental memory (same convention as the H1 Viterbi/DP/greedy smoke harness)
metadata.probabilistic_walk_next_finding: GraphPath.total_probability returned by Rhizomorph.probabilistic_walk_next is NOT a valid probability (it stores the raw un-normalized edge-weight product, not the normalized transition-probability product used internally for sampling); this benchmark recomputes baseline walk scores independently via _h2_recompute_path_probability rather than trusting that field
metadata.scope: exact-control tier ONLY (bubble graph + repeat-choice graph); realistic diploid and repeat-rich tiers are out of scope and unrun
metadata.score_definition: score = -log(total_probability); re-derived and re-sorted independently of k_shortest_paths's internal ordering, with tie-break (path length asc, joined-vertex string lexicographic, original enumeration index)
